articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

articles.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
articles.description: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offences

articles.title: Hyde Park shooting update
articles.description: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and his cousin Tobias Matonhodze are set to be sentenced on Wednesday after pleading guilty to charges linked to a Johannesburg shooting.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Air Commodore Bere declared national hero
more.description: Source: Air Commodore Bere declared national hero – herald Joseph Madzimure Zimpapers Politics Hub PRESIDENT Mnangagwa has conferred national hero status on Air Commodore (Retired) Simon Bere who died in Zambia, where he was receiving medical treatment, on April 24. He was 67. Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi, was sent by the [...] The post Air Commodore Bere declared national hero appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Workers deserve secure employment: President
more.description: Source: Workers deserve secure employment: President – herald Wallace Ruzvidzo Herald Reporter AS Zimbabwe joins the world in commemorating Workers’ Day, President Mnangagwa has issued a strong rebuke of perpetual short-term contracts, saying that where work is continuous, employment must be secure. In his May Day commemorative speech, the President said the practice of keeping [...] The post Workers deserve secure employment: President appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ED2030: Zanu PF ambushes villagers for signatures
more.description: Source: ED2030: Zanu PF ambushes villagers for signatures -Newsday Zimbabwe VILLAGERS in several parts of the country have raised alarm over a reported door-to-door campaign by Zanu PF structures demanding signatures in support of Constitutional Amendment No 3 Bill (CAB3). This is amid claims that the exercise is being used to pledge allegiance to President [...] The post ED2030: Zanu PF ambushes villagers for signatures appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’
more.description: Source: ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’ -Newsday Zimbabwe THE High Court has delivered a landmark ruling ordering the mandatory translation of all employment codes of conduct from English to every constitutionally recognised official language of the country, declaring the current English-only system a violation of fundamental workers’ rights. Justice Regis Dembure gave the [...] The post ‘Translate workplace codes to 16 official languages’ appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen
more.description: Source: Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen -Newsday Zimbabwe WORKERS across Zimbabwe commemorate International Workers’ Day today under a cloud of deepening economic hardships, with unions warning that deteriorating conditions, low wages and shrinking labour rights have stripped the day of its celebratory mood. This year’s commemorations come amid rising tensions in the labour [...] The post Workers’ Day gloomy as wages, conditions worsen appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: 2 400 nabbed in drug abuse crackdown
more.description: Source: 2 400 nabbed in drug abuse crackdown -Newsday Zimbabwe OVER 2 400 people have been nabbed in a nationwide crackdown on drug and substance abuse to curb the vice, which has been described as a growing public health and security concern. Public Service, Labour and Social Welfare acting director for drug and substance abuse, [...] The post 2 400 nabbed in drug abuse crackdown appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Mutare tax consultant Mahonye in dock over alleged company fraud
more.description: Source: Mutare tax consultant Mahonye in dock over alleged company fraud –Newsday Zimbabwe Mutare tax consultant Daniel Mahonye — also identified as Daniel Moyana — has been arrested on forgery allegations stemming from a mining company dispute. more.title: Zimbabwe crosses 1 000 mark in certified data guardians
more.description: Source: Zimbabwe crosses 1 000 mark in certified data guardians –Newsday Zimbabwe ZIMBABWE has trained just over 1 000 data protection officers (DPOs), marking a major step towards a digital economy. The statistics were revealed at the third National Data Privacy Symposium held in Bulawayo. It was revealed that 215 new DPOs were certified this [...] The post Zimbabwe crosses 1 000 mark in certified data guardians appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Deaf man gruesomely murders uncle after marriage proposal dispute
more.description: Source: Deaf man gruesomely murders uncle after marriage proposal dispute –Newsday Zimbabwe A 36-YEAR-OLD deaf man from Eastview, Harare, who fatally struck his uncle with a wheel spanner after the latter discouraged his [deaf man] girlfriend from marrying him due to his hearing impairment, will perform 420 hours of community service. more.title: No strings attached to China’s zero-tariff policy, says ambassador
more.description: Source: No strings attached to China’s zero-tariff policy, says ambassador -Newsday Zimbabwe CHINA’S ambassador to Zimbabwe, Zhou Ding, says there are no political strings to the zero-tariff offer extended to 53 African countries that have diplomatic ties with the Asian giant country. In an exclusive interview with NewsDay, the Chinese envoy said the zero-tariff policy, [...] The post No strings attached to China’s zero-tariff policy, says ambassador appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Exclusive - Presidential Borehole Scheme Brings Relief Across Political Divide
more.description: [263Chat] In a clear demonstration of President Emmerson Mnangagwa's commitment to inclusive governance, the Presidential Borehole Scheme is delivering clean water to communities across the political divide including areas traditionally considered opposition strongholds.
